slides.title: Too Many Else Ifs 😰
slides.content:
When checking a single variable against many potential values, writing 10 `else if` statements gets messy fast.

The `switch` statement is the cleaner alternative.

slides.title: A Clean Switch
slides.content: You provide a value to `switch`, and it looks for matching `case` values to run.

quizData.question: What happens if you omit the `break;` keyword inside a case?

options.text: It executes that case normally and stops.
It runs the matching case, and 'falls through' into the next cases below it.

quizData.explanation: Without `break`, the switch will continue executing the code in the subsequent cases below it.

slides.title: Default Fallback 🛡️
slides.content:
The `default` clause at the end is like the `else` block.

It runs only if absolutely none of the above `case` values matched.

slides.title: Strict Equality 🔒
slides.content: Note that `switch` uses strict equality (`===`) under the hood. The case `1` will not match a string `"1"`.
